text.title: AWS Infra Prompt Library Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `AWS Infra Prompt Library` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST live in a versioned file (git path + semver tag); inline prompts in editor history are not part of the library.
rule.rationale: Inline prompts are unreviewable + drift; versioned files give a single source of truth.

rule.statement: Each prompt MUST have a named human owner (handle + role) responsible for review + updates; group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned prompts rot; named owner enforces accountability.

rule.statement: Every prompt MUST include guardrails: `do not generate secrets`, `pin source-of-truth versions`, `output must be idempotent`, `flag missing IAM least-privilege`, `prefer Graviton + ARM where applicable`.
rule.rationale: Without guardrails, AI hallucinates secrets + uses `latest` tags + over-permissive IAM.

rule.statement: Prompt output MUST be reviewed by a human before being copy-pasted into IaC; auto-pasting from AI to repo is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Auto-pasted AI output has surfaced secrets + dropped IAM constraints in real audits.

rule.statement: The library MUST be reviewed quarterly: stale prompts (> 6 months) flagged, deprecated prompts archived, new categories proposed by team.
rule.rationale: Prompt corpora drift with AWS service evolution; quarterly review keeps the library aligned.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
